questions.q: What is the primary goal of management in an organisation?
questions.answer: To achieve organisational objectives efficiently and effectively

questions.q: Explain the concept of scientific management.
questions.answer: Scientific management is a theory of management that analyses and synthesises workflows. Its main objective is to improve economic efficiency, especially in labour productivity. It was first introduced by Frederick Winslow Taylor.

questions.q: State any two points of significance of management.
questions.answer: Two points of significance of management are: (i) Management helps in achieving group goals (ii) Management creates a dynamic environment

questions.q: Is management a science or an art? Give one reason in support of your answer.
questions.answer: Management is both a science and an art. One reason in support of this is that management has a systematic body of knowledge and principles which makes it a science.

questions.q: Identify the management function being performed by a manager when she is planning her department's budget for the next quarter.
What is the difference between management and administration?
questions.answer: Management refers to the process of planning, organising, staffing, directing and controlling. Administration refers to the activities that are performed by the top level of management.

questions.q: State any three characteristics of management.
questions.answer: Three characteristics of management are: (i) Management is a goal-oriented process (ii) Management is a continuous process (iii) Management is an intangible force

questions.q: Explain the importance of management in improving efficiency.
questions.answer: Management helps in improving efficiency by using optimum resources, eliminating wastages, and using resources for the right purpose at the right time. It also helps in the effective use of technology.

questions.q: State the importance of management in a society.
questions.answer: The importance of management in a society is that it helps in the development of the country by creating goods and services, by providing employment opportunities, and by improving the standard of living.
